content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

A subarray [AL,A(L+1),‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{(L+1)}, \ldots , A_R][AL‚Äã,A(L+1)‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã] (L<R)(L\lt R)(L<R) is said to be good if:


ALA_LAL‚Äã ‚à£|‚à£ A(L+1)A_{(L+1)}A(L+1)‚Äã ‚à£|‚à£ ‚Ä¶\ldots‚Ä¶ ‚à£|‚à£ ARA_RAR‚Äã >\gt> ALA_LAL‚Äã ‚äï\oplus‚äï A(L+1)A_{(L+1)}A(L+1)‚Äã ‚äï\oplus‚äï ‚Ä¶\ldots‚Ä¶ ‚äï\oplus‚äï ARA_RAR‚Äã

where ‚à£|‚à£ and ‚äï\oplus‚äï denote the bitwise or and xor operations respectively.
Find the number of good subarrays in the array.

Note that a subarray is obtained by deleting some (possibly zero) elements from the beginning and some (possibly zero) elements from the end of the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers  A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the number of good subarrays.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
